seoTitle: Deepdale vs Deepdene — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Deepdale and Deepdene. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Deepdale and Deepdene.
heading: Deepdale vs Deepdene — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Deepdale, Western Australia and Deepdene, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Deepdale has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Deepdene (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Deepdale or Deepdene safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Deepdale has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Deepdene scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Deepdale scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Deepdene scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Deepdale or Deepdene?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Deepdale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
